sources: arXiv:2602.12606 (RelBench v2 paper baselines: de facto SOTA for the 35 uncovered tasks)
arXiv:2411.19513 (ContextGNN), Kumo RFM tech report (rec numbers absent from the stale board)
_meta.notes: Classification values are test AUROC in percent. Board regression values are NMAE = MAE / std(train targets) — lower is better; per-task NMAE ranking is monotone with MAE. v2-paper regression values are R^2 — higher is better. Recommendation values are test MAP@K in percent.

rel-amazon/user-churn.note: Tight field (0.9pt spread over top-5); RDL baseline 70.4.

rel-amazon/user-ltv.note: Raw-MAE anchor: 14.226 (KumoRFM-ft). Heavy-tailed; GBDT on engineered aggregates is at parity with foundation models.

rel-amazon/item-ltv.note: Raw-MAE anchor 41.122. Feature engineering beats every GNN here.

rel-amazon/user-item-purchase.note: Official board is stale at 0.9 (two-tower). Hybrid pairwise+two-tower with shallow dst embeddings is the known recipe.

rel-amazon/review-rating.note: BOTH baselines are NEGATIVE R^2 — any sane user/item-history rating model should be a large jump. No external results published.

rel-avito/user-visits.note: 9-point outlier gap: the non-foundation field tops out at 69.4 — closing toward 78 is open territory.

rel-avito/user-clicks.note: Low-signal CTR task; feature search is competitive with GNNs.

rel-avito/ad-ctr.note: Agent-held record — beatable with bigger search + GNN ensembling. Raw-MAE anchor 0.034.

rel-avito/searchinfo-isuserloggedon.note: LightGBM at 50.0 — user-history features alone should do far better.

rel-event/user-attendance.note: Best-known EXCLUDES the board's sub-0.1 rows (RT-ft 0.0303, RT-zs 0.0662, PluRel 0.0708-0.0878): PROVENANCE VERIFIED 2026-07-17 — the RT paper (arXiv:2510.06377, p.6) states 'We skip rel-event, as it has been found to have temporal leakage issues' and never evaluated this cell, so those board rows are unsupported by the paper of record; PluRel's rows sit under the same leakage shadow pending its paper check. Raw-MAE anchor 0.235 (KumoRFM-2-ic). Our runs are leak-free by construction (sanitized cache truncates post-cutoff rows).

rel-event/event_interest-interested.note: Completely open: baselines are at/below coin-flip.

rel-event/users-birthyear.note: Negative R^2 baseline; social-graph age homophily (friends' ages) is the obvious untried signal.

rel-f1/driver-position.note: Raw-MAE anchor 2.731 (KumoRFM-ft) vs RDL 4.022 — 32% headroom over classic GNNs exists.

rel-f1/driver-circuit-compete.note: Only v2 baselines exist. Drivers race a fixed calendar — schedule/history heuristics should be extremely strong.

rel-f1/results-position.note: Only one external paper; qualifying data at the same date is highly predictive.

rel-hm/user-item-purchase.note: Kaggle-proven recipe: repeat-purchase + co-occurrence candidates + GBDT ranker.

rel-hm/transactions-price.note: Item's historical price nearly determines this — join-lookup in disguise.

rel-stack/badges-class.note: Badge Name column removed; class priors by badge-context are learnable.

rel-trial/study-outcome.note: Non-outlier bar ~72.5; LightGBM on target-table features already hits ~70 — text features are key.

rel-trial/site-success.note: Largest spread on the board; regression-head improvements (RelGNN 0.301 raw MAE vs RDL 0.400) land hard here.

rel-trial/condition-sponsor-run.note: Deep dst tower matters on this one (ContextGNN ablation).

rel-trial/site-sponsor-run.note: Hybrid pairwise+two-tower nearly doubled the board number.

rel-trial/studies-enrollment.note: v2 baselines are at zero — wide open.

rel-trial/eligibilities-adult.note: Criteria/age columns removed — the signal is in linked studies.

rel-arxiv/author-category.note: Soft: text (titles/abstracts of authored papers) barely exploited.

rel-arxiv/author-publication.note: Soft; author productivity autoregression is the obvious signal.

rel-salt/item-incoterms.note: Soft; likely a join-lookup problem (same customer's previous orders).

rel-salt/sales-group.note: VERY soft (LightGBM 0.75). High-cardinality lookup from customer/org history.

rel-salt/sales-payterms.note: VERY soft (LightGBM 0.47). Customer's previous payterms is the obvious feature.

rel-ratebeer/beer_ratings-total_score.note: Rating-prediction structure: user bias + item bias + interaction history.

rel-ratebeer/user-beer-favorite.note: Very soft; only v2 baselines exist.

rel-mimic/patient-iculengthofstay.note: Near-random baseline; clinical feature engineering should add 10+ points. Requires credentialed PhysioNet data.
